raw_text:
Sentinel Ridge Technologies

Enterprise security simplified through intelligent threat intelligence and
automated response

PRINCIPAL SECURITY ENGINEER

San Francisco, CA (Remote-Friendly) · Full-Time · $280,000 - $380,000 USD annually,

plus equity and performance bonus

We are seeking an exceptionally experienced Principal Security Engineer to

join our Security Engineering organization. In this role, you will define
and execute our organization-wide technical security strategy, architect
next-generation threat detection systems, and lead cross-functional

initiatives spanning application security, cloud infrastructure security,
and security operations. You will mentor senior engineers, drive technical

excellence, and shape the strategic direction of our security platform. This
is a hands-on leadership role ideal for someone who combines deep technical

expertise with the ability to influence organizational strategy and build
high-performing teams.

ABOUT US

Sentinel Ridge Technologies is a rapidly growing cybersecurity platform

company founded in 2018, specializing in enterprise security operations and
threat detection. We serve Fortune 500 companies, financial institutions,

and healthcare organizations across North America and Europe. Our mission is
to empower security teams with AI-driven threat intelligence and automated
response capabilities that reduce response time from hours to minutes.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

Define and evolve the organization-wide technical security strategy,
including architecture decisions for threat detection, SIEM integration,
and incident response automation across AWS and on-premises environments

Design and implement advanced threat modeling frameworks and AppSec
governance policies using OWASP guidelines, ensuring all product and
infrastructure components meet enterprise security standards

Lead the architecture and development of custom Python-based security
orchestration tools, SOAR workflows, and threat intelligence correlation
engines that integrate with commercial SIEM platforms

 Establish and maintain security-focused technical standards for cloud

infrastructure security, IAM policy frameworks, and secrets management
across AWS accounts and multi-tenant deployments

Conduct and oversee regular penetration testing campaigns, red team
engagements, and vulnerability assessments; partner with product and
infrastructure teams to prioritize and remediate findings

Mentor a team of senior and staff-level security engineers; provide
technical guidance on complex AppSec challenges, threat hunting
investigations, and security architecture decisions

Drive cross-team initiatives including the adoption of new SIEM
capabilities, enhancement of security automation playbooks, and
implementation of advanced threat modeling practices

Represent security engineering at the executive and board level;
communicate risk assessments, security roadmap priorities, and technical
recommendations to C-level stakeholders

Research emerging threat landscapes, evaluate new security tools and
frameworks, and guide technology decisions that balance innovation,

operational burden, and enterprise risk reduction

REQUIRED

12+ years of hands-on cybersecurity engineering experience, with at
least 5 years in a principal, staff, or senior architect role focused on
threat detection, application security, or security operations

Deep expertise in at least two of: SIEM architecture and tuning (Splunk,
ELK, or similar), advanced threat modeling methodologies (STRIDE, data

flow analysis), or cloud security architecture (AWS IAM, VPC, KMS,
CloudTrail)

Demonstrated expertise in Python or similar scripting languages for
security automation, threat intelligence integration, and custom tooling
development

Strong background in OWASP Top 10, secure SDLC, and application security

testing methodologies including code review and static/dynamic analysis

Proven experience designing and scaling security platforms or
infrastructure serving enterprise customers; understanding of security
operations workflows, incident response processes, and threat hunting

Excellent track record of mentoring senior-level engineers, driving

technical strategy across multiple teams, and influencing organizational
security decisions

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Cybersecurity, or related field
(or equivalent work experience); relevant certifications (CISSP, CCSK,
CEH, or OWASP certifications) are a plus

 NICE TO HAVE

Hands-on experience with penetration testing frameworks and tools; red
team or adversarial security exercise leadership

Published research, conference talks, or contributions to open-source
security projects demonstrating thought leadership in AppSec or threat
detection

Experience building security products or platform features used by
thousands of security professionals

Familiarity with compliance frameworks (SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI DSS) and
ability to translate compliance requirements into technical security
architecture

TECH STACK

Python 3.x   AWS IAM and Cloud Security   Splunk (SIEM)

OWASP Top 10 and Threat Modeling (STRIDE)   Penetration Testing Tools (Burp Suite, Metasploit)

AppSec Automation (SAST/DAST tools)   Kubernetes and Container Security

Terraform and Infrastructure-as-Code Security   Threat Intelligence Platforms (MITRE ATT&CK)

Security Orchestration Automation and Response (SOAR)

BENEFITS

Competitive salary and equity package with performance-based annual
bonuses

Comprehensive health insurance (medical, dental, vision) with company
contributions up to 90%

Unlimited PTO and 15 paid company holidays; parental leave (16 weeks)
and sabbatical opportunities

Annual professional development budget ($5,000) for certifications,

training, and conference attendance

401(k) matching (5% of salary) and financial planning services

Flexible work arrangements including remote work options and core hours
collaboration model

On-site amenities: free lunch and snacks, fitness center access,

wellness programs, and mental health support

HOW TO APPLY

Interested candidates should submit a resume, cover letter, and portfolio of
security projects or publications to careers@sentinelridge.io. Optionally,

include links to GitHub repositories, published research, or conference

 talks demonstrating your security expertise. Initial phone screening will be
conducted by our VP of Engineering; selected candidates will participate in

a technical deep-dive with our security leadership team. We review

applications on a rolling basis and aim to complete interviews within 3-4
weeks.
